lesson: A DRAW HANDLE OPEN alarm with the handle shut and the microswitch reading triggered means a bent actuator arm — a $12 switch, not the controller board.
symptoms: DRAW HANDLE OPEN alarm on the display.
Handle hasn't been touched — staff are adamant.
Display says the draw handle is open while it is fully at rest.
The alarm appeared immediately after cleaning.
Handle clicks shut normally, but the interlock remains active.
Controller will not start the serving cycle.
Staff cannot clear DRAW OPEN from the screen.

tests.error-log: An interlock fault latched since before open — the controller refuses to start until it clears.
tests.inspect-beater: The draw handle sits square and clicks home crisply — nothing bent or sloppy at the pivot.
tests.continuity-test: Draw microswitch reads closed — triggered — with the handle at rest.

flavour: Told the machine the door was open. The machine believed it.
authenticityNote: The draw handle microswitch can be bent by a heavy-handed pull or a dropped scoop. The controller reads 'handle open' and locks out dispensing — the whole machine appears dead until you trace it to this one $12 part.
